data.title: Forte Group Announces Strategic Initiatives to Strengthen Financial Position
data.description:
THIS NEWS RELEASE IS NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION TO U.S. NEWSWIRE SERVICES FOR DISSEMINATION IN THE UNITED STATES

VANCOUVER, BRITISH COLUMBIA / ACCESS Newswire / February 14, 2025 / Forte Group Holdings Inc. (CSE:FGH)(OTC:FGHFF)(FSE:7BC0, WKN:A40L1Z)("Forte Group" or the "Company"), a diversified lifestyle and wellness consumer packaged goods company, announces a non-brokered private placement financing (the "Private Placement"), consisting of the issuance of an aggregate of 200,000 common shares of the Company (the "Shares"), at a price of $0.60 per Share for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $120,000. The Shares will be subject to a statutory hold period expiring four months and one day from the date of issuance.

The Company intends to use the proceeds of the Private Placement for general working capital and outstanding payables. Closing of the Private Placement is anticipated to occur on or about February 24, 2025.

Insiders may participate in the Private Placement and will be considered a related party transaction subject to Multilateral Instrument 61-101 - Protection of Minority Security Holders in Special Transactions ("MI 61-101"). The Company intends to rely on exemptions from the formal valuation and minority shareholder approval requirements provided under subsections 5.5(a) and 5.7(a) of MI 61-101 on the basis that participation in the Private Placement by insiders will not exceed 25% of the fair market value of the Company's market capitalization. No finder's fees are payable in connection with the Private Placement.

This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy nor shall there be any sale of the securities in any state where such offer, solicitation, or sale would be unlawful. The securities being offered have not been, nor will they be, registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "1933 Act"), and none of them may be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from the registration requirements of the 1933 Act.

Proposed Conversion of Promissory Notes

The Company also announces that it intends to convert an aggregate principal amount of $29,000 in secured promissory notes secured against its property near Bridesville, British Columbia issued by Naturo Group into Common Shares to arm's length holders at a price of $0.48 per Common Share, for a total of 60,416 Common Shares (the "Shares for Debt Arrangement"). All Common Shares issued in connection with the Shares for Debt Arrangement will be subject to a restricted period of four months and one day from closing.

Closing of the Shares for Debt Arrangement is anticipated to be on or about February 24, 2025 in accordance with the policies of the Canadian Securities Exchange.

None of the securities issued pursuant to the Shares for Debt Arrangement have been or will be registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "1933 Act"), and none of them may be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from the registration requirements of the 1933 Act. This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy nor shall there be any sale of the securities in any state where such offer, solicitation, or sale would be unlawful.

About Forte Group Holdings Inc.

Forte Group Holdings Inc. (FSE:7BC0, WKN:A40L1Z) is a diversified lifestyle and wellness consumer packaged goods company. Forte Group develops and manufactures a range of alkaline and mineral-enriched beverages and nutraceutical supplements for both its TRACE brand and private-label clients. Based in British Columbia, Canada, Forte Group owns a pristine natural alkaline spring water aquifer and operates a 40,000-square-foot, Health Canada and HACCP-certified manufacturing facility near Osoyoos, British Columbia. The Company's distribution network includes traditional retail and e-commerce channels, delivering wellness-focused products directly to consumers through its innovative offerings.

data.title: Uber Technologies, Inc SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Uber Technologies, Inc., a global leader in ride-sharing, food delivery, and freight services, has released its annual Form 10-K report for the fiscal year 2024. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a highly competitive market. This summary highlights the key aspects of Uber's performance and strategic direction as outlined in the report.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $43,978 million, increased by 18% year-over-year, primarily driven by an increase in Mobility and Delivery Trip volumes.
  • Income from operations: $2,799 million, representing a 152% increase from the previous year, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net income attributable to Uber Technologies, Inc.: $9,856 million, significantly impacted by a $6.4 billion benefit from the release of valuation allowance on certain U.S. federal and state deferred tax assets.
  • Diluted EPS: Not explicitly stated in the provided sections.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Uber operates through three main segments: Mobility, Delivery, and Freight. Mobility revenue increased by 26% due to higher trip volumes, Delivery revenue grew by 13% driven by increased trip volumes and advertising revenue, while Freight revenue decreased by 2% due to lower revenue per load.
  • Geographical Performance: Uber's technology is available in over 70 countries, with significant operations in North America and Europe. Freight operations are primarily based in these regions, reflecting a strong presence in these markets.
  • Sales Units: The company reported a 19% increase in Trips, with Mobility and Delivery segments showing significant growth in trip volumes, indicating increased consumer engagement and platform usage.
  • New Product Launches: Uber has expanded its Delivery business to include Uber Direct, a white-label Delivery-as-a-Service offering, and introduced Uber Journey Ads as part of its advertising division.
  • Future Outlook: Uber plans to continue investing in new platform offerings to strengthen its existing services and enhance customer experience, aiming to attract new users and deepen engagement with existing ones.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Uber Technologies, Inc. has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its financial position and operational capabilities. The company has focused on improving its liquidity and capital structure by redeeming $2.0 billion of outstanding debt in the fourth quarter of 2024. Additionally, Uber has entered into an accelerated share repurchase agreement to buy back $1.5 billion of its common stock as part of its Share Repurchase Program. These actions are aimed at optimizing the capital structure and returning value to shareholders.
  • Capital Management: In 2024, Uber's capital management activities included the authorization of a $7.0 billion Share Repurchase Program, with $5.8 billion remaining available for future repurchases as of December 31, 2024. The company also repurchased $1.3 billion of common stock and redeemed $851 million of non-controlling interests. Furthermore, Uber issued $4.0 billion in term loans and notes, net of issuance costs, to refinance existing debt and support its capital management strategy. The company maintained a strong cash position with $7.0 billion in unrestricted cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments at year-end.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Uber plans to continue its strategic focus on debt reduction and share repurchases to enhance shareholder value. The company anticipates further optimizing its capital structure through potential prepayments of contested indirect tax assessments and other liquidity requirements. Uber is also monitoring the implementation of international tax changes, which may impact its future tax obligations. The company remains committed to evaluating opportunities for capital investment that align with its growth objectives and strategic priorities.

Challenges and Risks

  • Driver Classification: The company faces significant challenges related to the classification of Drivers as independent contractors. Legal proceedings globally, including class action lawsuits and government investigations, challenge this classification, which could lead to increased costs and a fundamental change in the business model if Drivers are reclassified as employees. This risk is compounded by legislative changes, such as California's Assembly Bill 5 and Proposition 22, and similar challenges in other jurisdictions like Massachusetts and Mexico.
  • Competitive Market: The mobility, delivery, and logistics industries are highly competitive, with low barriers to entry and well-capitalized competitors. The company must continuously lower fares, offer Driver incentives, and consumer discounts to remain competitive, which adversely affects financial performance. The presence of well-established alternatives and the propensity of users to switch to lower-cost providers further intensifies this risk.
  • Financial Stability: The company has incurred significant losses and expects operating expenses to increase, which may prevent it from maintaining profitability. Investments in new markets, technologies, and offerings are costly and may not yield expected returns, posing a risk to financial stability.
  • User Engagement: Maintaining a critical mass of platform users is essential, and failure to do so due to competition or other factors could reduce the platform's appeal, impacting financial results. The company also faces challenges in retaining high-quality personnel, with attrition and unsuccessful succession planning posing risks to business operations.
  • Brand and Reputation: The company's brand and reputation are critical, with negative media coverage and safety incidents potentially harming its ability to attract and retain users. The failure to offer competitive autonomous vehicle technologies could also impact financial performance and prospects.
  • International Operations: The company is substantially dependent on international operations, which present unique risks such as compliance challenges, regulatory differences, and political instability. These factors could adversely affect business operations and financial results.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which may adversely affect financial results. The reliance on credit card payments also poses a risk, as loss or modification of credit card acceptance privileges could significantly impact the business model.

data.title: LEAR CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Lear Corporation, a leading global supplier of automotive seating and electrical systems, has released its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year 2024. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the highly competitive automotive industry.

Financial Highlights

Lear Corporation reported the following key financial metrics for the fiscal year 2024:

  • Net sales: $23,306.0 million, reflecting a slight decrease from the previous year due to lower production volumes offset by new business.
  • Gross profit: $1,639.3 million, with a gross margin of 7.0%, impacted by lower production volumes and higher restructuring costs.
  • Consolidated income before provision for income taxes and equity in net income of affiliates: $732.9 million, showing a decrease due to lower gross profit and increased interest expense.
  • Net income attributable to Lear: $506.6 million, a decrease from the previous year, primarily due to lower gross profit and higher restructuring costs.
  • Diluted net income per share attributable to Lear: $8.97, reflecting the decrease in net income and a reduction in average diluted shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

Lear Corporation's business performance for 2024 included the following highlights:

  • Revenue Segments: The company's business is organized into two main segments: Seating and E-Systems. The Seating segment includes the design and manufacture of complete seat systems and key components, while the E-Systems segment focuses on electrical distribution and connection systems, high-voltage power distribution products, and electronic controllers.
  • Geographical Performance: Sales in North America increased by 3% compared to 2023, while sales in Europe and Africa decreased by 4%. Sales in Asia saw a slight decline of 1%, and South America experienced a 4% decrease.
  • Sales Units: The company reported a total light vehicle production volume of 88,133.9 thousand units in 2024, a 1% decrease from 2023. North America and Europe saw declines of 1% and 5%, respectively, while Asia remained stable.
  • New Product Launches: Lear has been focusing on expanding its product offerings in thermal comfort systems, including seat heating, ventilation, active cooling, and massage products, through acquisitions such as Kongsberg ICS and I.G. Bauerhin.
  • New Production Launches: The company has been investing in automation and advanced manufacturing technologies, including the acquisition of WIP Industrial Automation in 2024 and StoneShield Engineering in 2025, to enhance production efficiency and capabilities.
  • Future Outlook: Lear is positioned to capitalize on the trend of vehicle electrification, with investments in high-voltage power distribution products and connection systems. The company aims to extend its market leadership in Seating and transform its E-Systems business through growth in connection systems and vehicle architecture evolution.

Strategic Initiatives

Lear Corporation's strategic initiatives and capital management activities for 2024 include:

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is focused on extending its market leadership in Seating with modularity and thermal comfort systems, and transforming its E-Systems business through growth in connection systems and vehicle electrification. Investments in automation and digital technologies are aimed at enhancing operational excellence and profitability. Recent acquisitions, such as WIP Industrial Automation and I.G. Bauerhin, are aimed at strengthening robotics, AI capabilities, and thermal comfort systems, respectively.
  • Capital Management: Lear has a robust capital management strategy, including a $2.0 billion revolving credit facility and $2.6 billion in senior unsecured notes. The company repurchased $400 million of its common stock in 2024 and declared quarterly cash dividends of $0.77 per share. Additionally, Lear made a $50 million principal payment on its Term Loan and extended the maturity date of its revolving credit facility to 2027.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its focus on strategic growth through investments in automation and digital technologies. Lear aims to maintain a strong balance sheet and return capital to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. It also intends to align its manufacturing capacity with industry production levels and explore further restructuring actions to enhance operational efficiency.

Challenges and Risks

Lear Corporation faces several challenges and risks that could impact its financial performance:

  • Cyclical Industry Risks: The automotive industry is cyclical and sensitive to economic conditions, which can impact vehicle production levels and, consequently, the company's sales.
  • Supply Chain Risks: Increases in costs and restrictions on the availability of raw materials, energy, and labor could adversely affect financial performance. The company is also vulnerable to disruptions in its supply chain due to natural disasters or geopolitical events.
  • Customer and Product Risks: The lack of commercial success of vehicle models for which the company is a significant supplier, or increased directed component sourcing by customers, could reduce sales and margins.
  • Pricing Pressure: The company faces downward pricing pressure from automotive manufacturers, which requires it to achieve cost reductions to maintain profitability.
  • Trade Policy Risks: Changes in international trade policies, including tariffs and sanctions, could adversely affect financial performance. Recent announcements of new tariffs by the U.S. administration add uncertainty to the company's operations.
  • Labor Relations Risks: A significant labor dispute involving the company or its customers or suppliers could disrupt operations and adversely affect financial performance.
  • Technological and Cybersecurity Risks: The company is exposed to risks related to cybersecurity incidents, which could disrupt operations and lead to financial losses.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company must comply with various environmental and human rights regulations, which could result in significant costs and impact operations.

Management has identified several strategies to address these challenges, including diversifying sales across customers, products, and geographies, investing in product and process innovations, and enhancing operational efficiencies through automation and digital technologies. The company is also focusing on sustainability initiatives to drive business growth and improve employee retention.

data.title: HEALTHCARE SERVICES GROUP INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Healthcare Services Group Inc., a leading provider of housekeeping, laundry, and dietary management services to healthcare facilities, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report outlines the company's financial performance, operational highlights, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the healthcare services sector.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: The company reported revenues of $1,715.7 million, marking a 2.7% increase compared to the previous year. This growth was primarily driven by a 5.0% increase in Dietary services.

Costs of Services Provided: Costs of services provided were $1,487.6 million, an increase of 2.1%. However, these costs improved as a percentage of revenues to 86.7% from 87.2% in the previous year.

Net Income: Net income rose by 2.8% to $39.5 million, reflecting improved cost management.

Basic Earnings Per Common Share: Basic earnings per share increased to $0.54 from $0.52 in the previous year.

Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: Diluted earnings per share remained consistent at $0.53.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company is organized into two reportable segments: Housekeeping and Dietary. Housekeeping services accounted for approximately 44.6% of consolidated revenues, while Dietary services represented about 55.4% for the year ended December 31, 2024.

Operational Management Structure: The company operates through a structured management hierarchy, including Vice Presidents of Operations, Directors of Operations, District Managers, and Facility Managers, to ensure effective service delivery and customer satisfaction.

Customer Base: Healthcare Services Group provides services to approximately 2,600 healthcare facilities across the continental United States, including nursing homes, retirement complexes, rehabilitation centers, and hospitals.

Service Agreements: Services are primarily provided under full-service agreements, where the company manages day-to-day operations and supplies. A limited number of management-only agreements are also in place, where the company provides supervisory services while the customer retains payroll responsibility for non-supervisory staff.

Labor and Supply Costs: Housekeeping labor costs represented approximately 78.4% of Housekeeping revenues, while Dietary labor costs accounted for about 56.6% of Dietary revenues in 2024. Supply costs were 7.4% for Housekeeping and 32.5% for Dietary.

Significant Customers: Genesis Healthcare, Inc. was a significant customer, accounting for 8.7% of consolidated revenues in 2024. The company emphasizes the potential impact of losing such significant customers on its operations.

Market Trends: The market for services is expected to grow due to the aging U.S. population and government reimbursement policies requiring increased cost control in long-term and post-acute care facilities.

Employee Profile: As of December 31, 2024, the company employed approximately 35,300 people, with a focus on diversity and inclusion, as evidenced by a workforce consisting of 69% women and 62% BIPOC.

Health and Safety: The company is committed to employee health and safety, providing access to various health and welfare programs and ensuring compliance with environmental and safety regulations.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in the long-term care market and aims to maintain its position as a leading provider of housekeeping, laundry, and dietary management services in the U.S.

Strategic Initiatives

Financial Flexibility: The company has focused on enhancing its financial flexibility by maintaining a strong liquidity position, with cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaling $135.8 million as of December 31, 2024.

Investment in Technology: Healthcare Services Group has invested in a healthcare technology company, acquiring a 25% ownership stake to potentially leverage synergies in the long-term and acute care markets.

Capital Management: The company has actively managed its capital structure by repurchasing 0.4 million shares of its common stock for $5.0 million during the year ended December 31, 2024, under the authorized Repurchase Plan. Additionally, the company reduced its outstanding line of credit borrowings to zero by the end of 2024, down from $25 million at the end of 2023. The company also maintained a $300 million bank line of credit, with no borrowings as of December 31, 2024, providing significant financial flexibility.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, the company plans to continue its focus on maintaining a strong liquidity position and managing its capital structure prudently. The company expects to remain in compliance with its financial covenants and believes that its existing cash resources, along with the available line of credit, will be sufficient to fund operations and support growth initiatives over the next twelve months. The company also anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $5.0 million to $7.0 million in 2025, primarily for equipment and technology investments to support operational efficiency.

Challenges and Risks

Government Reimbursements: A significant challenge is the dependency on government reimbursements through Medicare and Medicaid, which can be affected by legislative changes impacting customer cash flows and, consequently, the company's revenue.

Macroeconomic Risks: The company faces several macroeconomic risks, including inflationary pressures and market fluctuations that affect the cost of labor and supplies. The reliance on a limited number of vendors for essential supplies poses a risk if these vendors fail to meet their obligations.

Health Crises: The company is vulnerable to pandemics and other health crises, which can disrupt operations and increase costs.

Geopolitical Tensions and Natural Disasters: These factors pose risks to the company's operations and financial stability.

Regulatory Changes: Changes in healthcare legislation could increase operating costs and affect profitability.

Cybersecurity Risks: The company is exposed to cybersecurity risks, having experienced a cyber-attack in 2024, highlighting the need for robust data protection measures.

Operational Performance: The company has identified the need to manage labor costs and supply chain disruptions as critical to maintaining operational performance. The bankruptcy of a significant customer, LaVie Care Centers, has increased bad debt provisions, impacting financial results. Management is focused on optimizing credit and collections efforts to mitigate risks associated with customer financial difficulties.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks related to interest rate fluctuations, which can affect investment returns and borrowing costs. The reliance on marketable securities for liquidity and balance sheet support means that declines in market prices could adversely impact financial results. The company maintains a line of credit to manage liquidity needs, but changes in market conditions could affect access to capital.

data.title: Elme Communities SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Elme Communities, a Maryland-based real estate investment trust (REIT) specializing in apartment communities in the Washington, DC metro and Sunbelt regions, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report details the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $223.6 million, increased by 6.8% from 2023 primarily due to higher rental income and the acquisition of Elme Druid Hills.
  • Net Operating Income (NOI): $153.2 million, increased by 3.5% from 2023, driven by higher rental rates and the acquisition of Elme Druid Hills.
  • Net Loss: $13.1 million, a significant improvement from a net loss of $53.0 million in 2023, primarily due to the absence of real estate impairment charges and transformation costs.
  • NAREIT Funds From Operations (FFO): $82.8 million, increased by 6.4% from 2023, reflecting higher NOI and lower transformation costs.
  • Residential Revenue: $213.7 million, increased by 3.3% from 2023, primarily due to higher rental income and recoveries.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Elme Communities focuses on owning apartment communities in the Washington, DC metro and Sunbelt regions. As of December 31, 2024, the company owned 28 apartment communities and one office property.
  • Investment Strategy: The company aims to provide quality, affordably priced housing targeting middle-income renters. It focuses on markets with diverse, innovative industries that drive job creation and wage growth, and executes value-add renovations to enhance shareholder value.
  • Geographical Performance - Washington, DC Metro: The region absorbed 8,200 units in Q4 2024, with occupancy increasing to 96.0%. Annual rent growth was 3.4%, with Class A properties experiencing the highest growth at 4.2%.
  • Geographical Performance - Atlanta Metro: The region absorbed 7,600 units in Q4 2024, with occupancy at 92.8%. However, the area saw an annual rent cut of 4.1%, with Class C properties experiencing the steepest decline.
  • Portfolio Details: As of December 31, 2024, Elme Communities owned approximately 9,400 residential apartment homes and 300,000 square feet of commercial space. The residential properties had an average occupancy of 95% for the year.
  • Operational Changes: In 2023, Elme Communities completed the internalization of its management operations, transitioning all residential communities to Elme management by July 2023.
  • New Initiatives: In 2024, the company launched Elme Resident Services to streamline community operations and enhance customer service across its multifamily portfolio.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects to benefit from sustained demand for affordable rental options due to high homeownership costs and plans to continue focusing on mid-market price points that do not compete with new supply.
  • Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) Initiatives: Elme Communities is committed to sustainable operations, including energy-efficient upgrades and a goal of net zero carbon operation by 2050. Social initiatives include financial inclusion programs and health and wellness amenities.
  • Human Capital: As of February 11, 2025, the company employed 255 people, with a focus on employee growth, training, and development. The company supports diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility through its Cultural Advisory Board.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Transformation: The company has undertaken a strategic transformation to focus on residential operations, including the acquisition of Elme Druid Hills and the insourcing of property-level management activities. Additionally, the company launched Elme Resident Services to centralize and enhance customer service across its multifamily portfolio. The Board has initiated a formal review to evaluate strategic alternatives to maximize shareholder value.
  • Capital Management: In 2024, the company entered into a third amended and restated credit agreement, increasing its revolving loan commitments to $500 million with an accordion feature up to $1 billion. The company also executed forward interest rate swap arrangements to manage interest rate exposure. A share repurchase program of up to $50 million was authorized, and no common shares were issued under the equity distribution agreement in 2024. The company maintains a dividend reinvestment program and did not issue preferred shares as of December 31, 2024.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects to have adequate liquidity to meet its obligations and fund growth opportunities, with no debt maturities until 2026. It plans to invest $41.0 - $46.0 million in its existing portfolio and fund potential acquisitions in 2025. The company will continue to assess dividend payments quarterly and explore capital sources such as equity offerings and joint ventures to support its strategic initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Expansion Risks: Elme Communities faces significant challenges in expanding its operations into new markets, particularly the Sunbelt region, due to a lack of familiarity with local market dynamics compared to its established presence in the Washington, DC metro area. This expansion strategy introduces risks related to market entry and integration of new acquisitions.
  • Strategic Review Process: The company is undergoing a strategic review process initiated by the Board, which could adversely affect its business operations and stock price due to uncertainties and potential disruptions.
  • Concentration Risks: The concentration of investments in the multifamily asset class and specific geographic regions (Washington, DC and Atlanta) exposes the company to market-dependent risks, including economic downturns and regulatory changes.
  • Macroeconomic Risks: Macroeconomic trends such as inflation and high interest rates pose risks to cash flow and financial condition, potentially increasing operating and borrowing costs.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces operational risks related to short-term leases, which expose it to market rent fluctuations more quickly than long-term leases. The potential for increased expenses, even if revenues decline, is a concern, as fixed costs like loan payments remain constant.
  • Development Risks: Risks associated with property development and redevelopment, including permitting, financing, and construction delays, could adversely impact financial performance.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with rent control and stabilization laws may limit the ability to increase rents and affect profitability. Environmental regulations and climate change pose additional challenges, potentially leading to increased costs and operational restrictions.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The company is exposed to cybersecurity risks, which could disrupt operations and compromise confidential information.
  • Interest Rate Risks: Elme Communities is exposed to interest rate risks due to its reliance on variable rate debt. The company employs hedging strategies to mitigate these risks, but these strategies carry their own risks, such as counterparty performance and legal enforceability. The potential for increased interest expenses due to rising rates could adversely affect cash flow and financial condition.
